author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXXIV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold, now, bless ye the Lord, all ye servants of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1), “who stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why has he added, “in the courts”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Courts mean the wider spaces of a house.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who stands in the courts is not straitened, is not confined, in some fashion is enlarged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Remain in this enlargement, and thou canst love thy enemy, because thou lovest not things in which an enemy could straiten thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How canst thou be understood to stand in the courts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Stand in charity, and thou standest in the courts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Breadth lies in charity, straitness in hatred.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lift up your hands by night in the sanctuary, and bless the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is easy to bless by day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “by day”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In prosperity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For night is a sad thing, day a cheerful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When it is well with thee, thou dost bless the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy son was sick, and he is made whole, thou dost bless the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy son was sick, perchance thou hast sought an astrologer, a soothsayer, perchance a curse against the Lord has come, not from thy tongue, but from thy deeds, from thy deeds and thy life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Boast not, because thou blessest with thy tongue, if thou cursest with thy life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore bless ye the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When did Job bless?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When it was a sad night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All was taken away which he possessed; the children for whom his goods were stored were taken away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How sad was his night!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us however see whether he blesseth not in the night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord gave, the Lord hath taken away; it is as the Lord willed; blessed be the name of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And black was the night.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord out of Zion bless thee, who made heaven and earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He exhorts many to bless, and Himself blesseth one, because He maketh one out of many, since “it is good and pleasant for brethren to dwell together in one.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a plural number, brethren, and yet singular, to dwell together in one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none of you say, It cometh not to me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Knowest thou of whom he speaks, “the Lord bless thee out of Zion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He blessed one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be one, and the blessing cometh to thee.
